LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT NICOLE SCHOTT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 25, 2012. THE MOVING PARTY MUST PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 BY 4PM ON JUNE 18 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
ORDER RE: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT NICOLE SCHOTT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SATISFIED THE FIRST PRONG OF THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE BY SHOWING THAT HER REVIEW IS PROTECTED SPEECH BECAUSE IT WAS MADE IN A PUBLIC FORUM AND ADDRESSES ISSUES OF PUBLIC CONCERN. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO SHOW THE REQUIRED MINIMUM MERIT TO EITHER OF THEIR CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES POST-APPEAL IS CONTINUED FROM FEB-23-2015 TO MAR-23-2015, AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPT. 302. THE OPPOSING PARTY FILED A DECLARATION STATING THAT HE DID NOT RECEIVE THE MOVING PAPERS. THE MOVING PARTY SHALL IMMEDIATELY SERVE BY PERSONAL OR BY E-MAIL SERVICE BY FEBRUARY 23, 2015, THE MOTION ON THE DEFENDANT. THE OPPOSITION IS DUE ON MARCH 10, 2015, AND THE REPLY IS DUE ON MARCH 17, 2015. ALL PARTIES TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES PER SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302. MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. J. MICHAEL ANTHONY, Tel No. (415) 485-2200, appearing on behalf of the Plaintiffs;
Atty. IAN BOOTH KELLEY, Tel No. (415) 581-0889, appearing on behalf of Defendant Nicole Schott.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NICOLE SCHOTT'S Motion For Attorney Fees Post-Appeal is granted. The court awards $37,985 as reasonable fees and costs per CCP 425.16(c). ORDER AND JUDGMENT BOTH SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees Post-Appeal
Off Calendar Feb-23-2015 Continued To Mar-23-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NICOLE SCHOTT'S Motion For Attorney Fees Post-Appeal is continued to March 23, 2015. The opposing party filed a declaration stating that he did not receive the moving papers. The moving party shall immediately serve by personal or by e-mail service by February 23, 2015, the motion on the defendant. The opposition is due on March 10, 2015, and the reply is due on March 17, 2015. All parties to provide courtesy copies per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recover Fees And Costs Under The Antislapp Statute
Off Calendar Sep-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RUSSELL ROBINSON, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF RUSSELL ROBINSON, 415.255.0462, FOR PLAINTIFF BOB KORMAN.
IAN BOOTH KELLEY, ESQ., 415.581.0889, FOR DEFNEDNAT NICOLE SCHOTT.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT NICOLE SCHOTT'S Motion To Recover Fees And Costs Under The Antislapp Statute is granted in part. All costs are denied. All fees are awarded at an hourly rate of $300 without a multiplier. Order signed in open Court. JUDGMENT signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ian Kelly, Esq., of the Law Office of Ian Kelly, Tel. 415.581.0885, appearing for defendant and moving party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NICOLE SCHOTT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Defendant satisfied the first prong of the anti-SLAPP statute by showing that her review is protected speech because it was made in a public forum and addresses issues of public concern. Plaintiffs failed to show the required minimum merit to either of their claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

The Court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
